invariants: Exactly one selected sleeve and one selected source exist per plan.
Selected source must match the sleeve binding and executor binding source.
Budget, monitor, and executor bindings are fully resolved before the plan starts.
Pinned commit must match the selected executor profile and frozen source reference.
No emitted plan may mutate route, sleeve, budget, monitor, or executor binding in place.

resolution_rules: Every objective profile resolves to exactly one preferred source.
Mixed or unspecified objectives resolve to Dexter.
Any Mew-X profile explicitly accepts timeout-shaped lifecycle behavior.
Objective profiles may not authorize multi-source sleeves or in-run source fusion.

invariants: One plan binds exactly one sleeve.
A sleeve binds to exactly one source.
No plan may rebind between Dexter and Mew-X after start.
Mew-X sleeve enablement is always explicit.
Any Mew-X share is explicit and never implicit.
Mixed or unspecified objectives cannot emit a nonzero Mew-X budget by default.
No budget policy implies dynamic cross-sleeve rebalance inside one immutable plan.

binding_rules: Every plan binds exactly one monitor profile.
Admission gates can block config invalidity, pin mismatch, disabled sleeve, budget overflow, or active global halt.
Global halt freezes new planning and dispatches source-native stop requests only.
mewx_timeout_boundary: A normal inactivity_timeout close reason is source-native and not an automatic incident.
Quarantine triggers only when the session outlives the configured timeout envelope or fails to emit normalized close or shutdown confirmation.
Mew-X quarantine must not transfer the position to Dexter.

invariants: Pinned commit matches the frozen source reference.
Executors receive immutable plan data rather than mutable route instructions.

why: The immutable plan schema, route invariants, and binding references are now fixed strongly enough to implement planner-side validation and plan emission directly.
Executor boundary is already fixed at the contract level, so implementation can focus on config loading, route resolution, immutable plan construction, and dispatch orchestration.
Monitor thresholds can attach to the shared plan and status surface afterward without creating a parallel contract.
Numeric timeout and quarantine thresholds remain implementation defaults rather than evidence decisions.
The monitoring spec should attach to the concrete planner lifecycle and plan registry rather than define that lifecycle indirectly.
The source-faithful boundary is already fixed here through methods, statuses, source pins, and ownership split.
What remains open is transport and process model, which is implementation-local rather than the next evidence-bound spec cut.

residual_and_caveats.replay_scope: Current zero-gap evidence is scoped to close-summary and position_closed realized-return fidelity rather than full execution-path equivalence.

dexter_metric_scope_gap.why_it_matters: A stronger claim about path-sensitive retention still requires a separate lane.

residual_and_caveats.candidate_surface_gap: Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy remains unavailable because the frozen export surface does not expose a comparable creator_candidate path.
residual_and_caveats.planner_router_spec_boundary: Dexter remains the default execution anchor, Mew-X remains the bounded selective option for containment-first objectives, and numeric budget caps, numeric monitor thresholds, and transport details remain later defaults.

why: The fixed comparison, execution, and implementation layers are now explicit enough to freeze one immutable planner/router contract.
Dexter remains the default execution backbone for the broader operational objective set.
Mew-X still has a real but bounded role for containment-first sleeves without upgrading into fusion or fallback behavior.
That is sufficient to move directly into a concrete planner/router implementation spec without reopening comparison work.
